evidence_summary: The primary causes of training instability in reinforcement learning agents after policy updates are not explicitly mentioned in the provided evidence.

Proximal policy optimization (PPO) is proposed as a new family of policy gradient methods for reinforcement learning, which can alternate between sampling data and optimizing a surrogate objective function.
PPO has some benefits of trust region policy optimization (TRPO), but is simpler to implement, more general, and has better sample complexity empirically.
The evidence does not provide information on how to effectively mitigate learning rate sensitivity in PPO and Adam algorithms.
